en: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：

en: "Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them, You shall be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy.
zh: “你要告訴以色列全體會眾，對他們說：你們要分別為聖，因為我耶和華你們的 神是聖潔的。

en: Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father, and you shall keep my Sabbaths: I am the LORD your God.
zh: 各人要孝敬父母，也要謹守我的安息日；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves any gods of cast metal: I am the LORD your God.
zh: 你們不可轉向偶像，也不可為自己鑄造神像；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: "When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD, you shall offer it so that you may be accepted.
zh: “如果你們獻平安祭給耶和華，要使你們所獻的蒙悅納。

en: It shall be eaten the same day you offer it or on the day after, and anything left over until the third day shall be burned up with fire.
zh: 祭牲的肉，要在你們獻的那一天和第二天吃；如果有剩下的，到了第三天，就要用火燒掉。

en: If it is eaten at all on the third day, it is tainted; it will not be accepted,
zh: 如果在第三天吃平安祭肉，這祭肉已經成為不潔淨，不蒙悅納。

en: and everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity, because he has profaned what is holy to the LORD, and that person shall be cut off from his people.
zh: 那吃祭肉的人，要擔當自己的罪責，因為他褻瀆了耶和華的聖物；那人必從自己的族人中被剪除。

en: "When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap your field right up to its edge, neither shall you gather the gleanings after your harvest.
zh: “你們收割莊稼的時候，不可把角落的穀物都割盡，也不可拾取收割時遺下的。

en: And you shall not strip your vineyard bare, neither shall you gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard. You shall leave them for the poor and for the sojourner: I am the LORD your God.
zh: 不可把你葡萄園的果子都摘盡，也不可拾取你葡萄園中掉下的；要把它們留給窮人和寄居的外人；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: "You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; you shall not lie to one another.
zh: “你們不可偷竊，不可欺騙，不可彼此說謊。

en: You shall not swear by my name falsely, and so profane the name of your God: I am the LORD.
zh: 不可奉我的名起假誓，褻瀆你 神的名；我是耶和華。

en: "You shall not oppress your neighbor or rob him. The wages of a hired servant shall not remain with you all night until the morning.
zh: “不可欺壓你的鄰舍，也不可搶奪他的東西；雇工的工錢，不可扣留在你那裡到早晨。

en: You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind, but you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
zh: 不可咒罵聾子，也不可把絆腳石放在瞎子面前；卻要敬畏你的 神；我是耶和華。

en: "You shall do no injustice in court. You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great, but in righteousness shall you judge your neighbor.
zh: “你們審判的時候，不可行不義；不可偏袒窮人，也不可偏幫有權勢的人；只要按著公義審判你的鄰舍。

en: You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not stand up against the life of your neighbor: I am the LORD.
zh: 你不可在你的族人中，到處搬弄是非，也不可危害你的鄰舍 ；我是耶和華。

en: "You shall not hate your brother in your heart, but you shall reason frankly with your neighbor, lest you incur sin because of him.
zh: “你不可心裡恨你的兄弟；應坦誠責備你的鄰舍，免得你因他擔當罪過。

en: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the LORD.
zh: 不可報復，也不可向你的族人懷恨，卻要愛你的鄰舍好像愛自己；我是耶和華。

en: "You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your cattle breed with a different kind. You shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed, nor shall you wear a garment of cloth made of two kinds of material.
zh: “你們要謹守我的律例。不可使你的牲畜與不同類的交合；在你的田裡不可撒兩種不同類的種子；兩樣料子作成的衣服，你不可穿在身上。

en: "If a man lies sexually with a woman who is a slave, assigned to another man and not yet ransomed or given her freedom, a distinction shall be made. They shall not be put to death, because she was not free;
zh: 如果人與女子同睡交合，而那女子是個婢女，已經許配了人，但還沒有贖身，或是還沒有得著自由，就應受懲罰，但不可把二人處死，因為婢女還沒有得著自由。

en: but he shall bring his compensation to the LORD, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram for a guilt offering.
zh: 那人要把他的贖愆祭，就是一隻作贖愆祭的公綿羊，帶到會幕門口，耶和華面前。

en: And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin that he has committed, and he shall be forgiven for the sin that he has committed.
zh: 祭司要用那隻作贖愆祭的公綿羊，在耶和華面前，為他所犯的罪贖罪，他所犯的罪必蒙赦免。

en: "When you come into the land and plant any kind of tree for food, then you shall regard its fruit as forbidden. Three years it shall be forbidden to you; it must not be eaten.
zh: “你們進了那地，栽種了各樣可作食物的果樹的時候，你們要視初結的果子為不潔淨，好像未受割禮的人。頭三年的果子，你們不可以吃。

en: And in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy, an offering of praise to the LORD.
zh: 第四年所結的一切果子，應分別為聖，是作稱頌耶和華的供物。

en: But in the fifth year you may eat of its fruit, to increase its yield for you: I am the LORD your God.
zh: 到了第五年，你們才吃樹上所結的果子，你們這樣作，你們的生產就必增加；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: "You shall not eat any flesh with the blood in it. You shall not interpret omens or tell fortunes.
zh: “你們不可吃帶血的東西，不可占卜，不可算命。

en: You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
zh: 不可剃兩邊的頭髮，鬍鬚的周圍也不可刮掉。

en: You shall not make any cuts on your body for the dead or tattoo yourselves: I am the LORD.
zh: 不可為死人的緣故，割傷你們的身體，也不可在你們的身上刺花紋；我是耶和華。

en: "Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute, lest the land fall into prostitution and the land become full of depravity.
zh: “不可辱沒你的女兒，使她作娼妓，免得這地淫亂，充滿醜惡的事。

en: You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
zh: 你們要謹守我的安息日，敬畏我的聖所；我是耶和華。

en: "Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out, and so make yourselves unclean by them: I am the LORD your God.
zh: “你們不可轉向那些交鬼的和行法術的；不可求問他們，玷污自己；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: "You shall stand up before the gray head and honor the face of an old man, and you shall fear your God: I am the LORD.
zh: “在白髮的人面前，你要起立；要尊敬老年人，又要敬畏你的 神；我是耶和華。

en: "When a stranger sojourns with you in your land, you shall not do him wrong.
zh: “如果有外人在你們的地方，與你們一起寄居，你們不可欺負他。

en: You shall treat the stranger who sojourns with you as the native among you, and you shall love him as yourself, for you were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
zh: 與你們一起寄居的外人，要看他像你們中間的本地人一樣；你要愛他好像愛自己，因為你們在埃及地也曾作過寄居的人；我是耶和華你們的 神。

en: "You shall do no wrong in judgment, in measures of length or weight or quantity.
zh: “你們審判的時候，在度、量、衡上，都不可偏差。

en: You shall have just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
zh: 要用公正的天平，公正的法碼，公正的升斗，公正的容器；我是耶和華你們的 神，就是曾把你們從埃及地領出來的那位。

en: And you shall observe all my statutes and all my rules, and do them: I am the LORD."
zh: 你們要謹守遵行我的一切律例和一切典章；我是耶和華。”
